zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Smythe於1414年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾諾維爾時，她的父親Sir John Carrington Smith44歲，母親Joan Willoughby43歲。她於1441年與Sir William Cromwell在英國諾廷加姆爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1467年4月14日在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾卡爾頓 奧 特倫特享年53歲。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Smythe was born in 1414, in Norwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Carrington Smith, was 44 and her mother, Joan Willoughby, was 43. She married Sir William Cromwell in 1441, in Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 14 April 1467, in Carlton on Trent, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Smythe est née en 1414, à Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Carrington Smith, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Joan Willoughby, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Cromwell en 1441, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 14 avril 1467, à Carlton on Trent, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Smythe(이)가 1414년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 노웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Carrington Smith(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Willoughby(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1441년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 Sir William Cromwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1467년 4월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 칼턴  트렌트에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.
